Parth Parmar
About Resume Blog

Hi, I'm Parth Parmar.

I’m a software engineer focused on building infrastructure for large-scale AI systems. My work sits at the intersection of distributed systems and machine learning infrastructure.

I’m fascinated by the engineering challenges behind modern AI systems — how models move from research prototypes to reliable production systems that millions of users interact with every day. Much of my work focuses on the infrastructure that makes this possible: evaluation systems, data generation pipelines, and scalable platforms for deploying AI capabilities.

I enjoy working on problems where machine learning meets systems engineering. Many of the challenges in AI today are not purely about better models, but about building the infrastructure around them — systems that are scalable, observable, and reliable enough to support real-world applications. This website is a place where I document what I’m learning.

Currently Exploring..

  • Model serving and inference systems
  • Data pipelines and orchestration
  • Platform reliability and observability

Understanding LLM Inference by Building a Server From Scratch

March 14, 2026 - Platform, Serving

A closer look at what happens inside an LLM inference server, from prompt arrival to streamed tokens, with vLLM as the serving baseline.
